parsed_review.summary: CP00-185 is a small Risk A, descriptor-only closeout pack binding the RP05.P05 permission/audit tail plus base tenant fixture (10 units, RP05.P05.M05.S14-RP05.P05.M06.S01). Independent read-only review across all 8 implementation refs, 5 evidence files, the closeout plan, and the review harness found the pack internally consistent and honest: production_ready is false and the Hermes (H05) and Claude (C05) gates are pending everywhere (manifest, command-evidence, adjudication, construction-inspection, claude-review-result), which is the CORRECT pre-review state rather than a premature/false readiness claim. The descriptor-level production_ready_candidate is true because validateMatterCoreCp185Coverage (distribution fixture:2/test:3/hermes_evidence:1/implementation:4; phase RP05.P05=10; micro M05=9/M06=1) and validateMatterCoreCp185PermissionAuditTail (source inheritance CP184/CP183/CP180; 10 tail rows; failure_test=blocked; claude_missing_test_prompt=review_required; synthetic base tenant; all leak guards) both pass in source — but final readiness correctly remains gated. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via MATTER_CORE_CP185_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and the freeze stamp (all write/runtime/leak flags false; synthetic_only true), and the explicit CP00-185 plan entry (closeout-pack-plan.json:414) matches the binding, manifest snapshot, and contract exactly. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: the readiness auth-preflight returned a non-empty 1458-byte valid-JSON success (exit 0) — not auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped — and invalid_review_blockers wires the forbidden-review-evidence list into the C05 packet. Claude is held as a read-only independent reviewer and NOT final approver (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, claude_is_final_approval=false across registry/service/contract/manifest, asserted by tests and the validate script), and Hermes appears only as a deterministic control-plane evidence/review-packet pattern (H05.CP00-185…, C05.CP00-185…) with graph/citation/loop runtime flags all false — not embedded in product runtime. Unrelated dirty/untracked files are explicitly preserved as out-of-scope (active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty). No P0/P1/P2 defects; two P3 informational notes record the read-only execution caveat and the by-design empty command-evidence so the human final approver has full context. This review does not constitute final approval or any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: Read-only review verifies validation logic by inspection, not execution
findings.details: As mandated, this review used only Read/Grep/Glob and did not run Bash, so the 11 baseline validation commands (npm test, node scripts/validate-rp05-matter-core-contract.mjs, etc.) were not executed. The PASS is grounded in reading the source that asserts the invariants: validate-rp05-matter-core-contract.mjs:371-388 asserts cp185Coverage.valid, cp185PermissionAuditTail.valid, hermes.production_ready_candidate===true, claude.read_only with allowed_tools [Read,Grep,Glob], handoff to CP00-186/RP05.P05.M06.S02, tail_row_count 10, failure_test blocked, claude_missing_test_prompt review_required, and all leak_guards true; packages/matter/test/model.test.js:1502-1590 mirrors these. Execution-time confirmation is the Hermes H05 gate's responsibility, which is correctly still pending.
findings.recommendation: Human final approver / Hermes H05 gate should execute the recorded validation commands and capture their results before flipping production_ready, since this read-only Claude gate cannot and does not execute them.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_no_action — expected property of the read-only review modality; execution is delegated to the H05 gate prior to closeout.

findings.title: command-evidence.json records no executed commands at review time (by design)
findings.details: command-evidence.json has commands: [] with hermes_gate H05 and claude_gate C05 both 'pending' and gate_outcome 'pending'. This is consistent with — not contradictory to — the pack's production_ready:false pre-review state: the gates have not yet run, so no command transcripts are expected. It is called out only so a downstream reader does not misread the empty array as missing/lost evidence.
findings.recommendation: Populate command-evidence.commands and flip the H05/C05 gate statuses during closeout after this review and command execution; keep production_ready false until then.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_no_action — empty commands array is the correct state for a pack awaiting its gates; no change required for this review to pass.
